item.title: NLP Series [Part 3] · LSTM-Language-Model
item.description: Such models are called “Language Models”, trained to recurrently produce text. We can also use such models to assign probabilites to pieces of text. We will be training a character level LSTM but it can be trained at word level as well(vocabulary size will be much higher in case of words). Within each forward pass, the model will receive indices for a sequence and will be tasked to predict the next character at each time step.

item.title: NLP Series [Part 2] · Word2Vec-Skip-Gram-Negative-Sampling
item.description: Most of the steps are similar to our notebook on Word2Vec without negative sampling. The key changes happen with the dataset where we provide a list of indices for negative sample for each forward pass as well as the loss function. In this notebook, we will train a Word2Vec skip-gram model with negative sampling from scratch on some text and inspect the trained embeddings at the end. The architecture we define later will enable us to learn a distributed representation for each word/token in our corpus.

item.title: NLP Series [Part 1] · Word2Vec-Skip-Gram
item.description: In this notebook, we will train a Word2Vec skip-gram model from scratch on some text and inspect the trained embeddings at the end. The architecture we define later will enable us to learn a distributed representation for each word/token in our corpus. The key idea being, we can represent a word by adding contextual information to it. Context here is the neighbouring words around a word/token of interest to us.

item.title: MLOps template [Part 4] · FastAPI + ElasticAPM
item.description: Scripts related to the APIs are located at MLOps/app. ├── application.py # FastAPI app and launcher uvicorn.run ├── models # pydantic model for reponse validation │ └── predict.py # pydantic model for predict endpoint └── routes # to maintain larger applications ├── endpoints │ └── predict.py # predict endpoint └── router.py # APIRouter module to maintain larger apps Though we have used the APIRouter module here, it’s really needed when we have lots of API endpoints in a larger app.

item.title: MLOps template [Part 3] · MLflow + MinIO
item.description: @pipeline def ml_pipeline(): # 1. fetch training data texts, target = get_training_dataset() # 2. minimal text preprocessing # 3. tfidf vectorization vectorizer, X = get_vectorizer_and_features(preprocess_text(texts)) # 4. target encoding target_encoder, encoded_target = get_targetencoder_and_encoded_targets(target) # 5. train test split X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, encoded_target) # 6. model training, validation, registry, artifact storage train_clf(X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test) MLflow # # MLflow is an open source platform for managing the end-to-end machine learning lifecycle.

item.title: MLOps template [Part 2] · Dagster Pipeline
item.description: Dagster is a data orchestrator for machine learning, analytics, and ETL Dagster is a second generation data orchestrator that focues on being data-driven rather than task-driven(like Airflow). Solids and Pipelines # # Dagster’s core abstractions are solids and pipelines. Solids are individual units of computation that we wire together to form pipelines. By default, all solids in a pipeline execute in the same process. In production environments, Dagster is usually configured so that each solid executes in its own process.

item.title: MLOps template [Part 1] · Setup
item.description: These steps are necessary for operationalization of any machine-learning based model. These posts are in no way exhaustive in covering the breadth of MLOps. Several key pieces like the CI/CD pipeline, monitoring for drift, etc are missing at the moment, which might get added later. Stack # We will be using the following tools in this project Data Pipeline: Dagster ML registry: MLflow API Development: FastAPI Monitoring: ElasticAPM Dev Setup # Poetry # # $ curl -sSL https://raw.
